meaning: adj.可能的,想得到的,可想像的
examples: It is conceivable that I'll see her tomorrow.
Milton uses the language of these two parables to get at this problem that has resonances in every conceivable sphere.
It is conceivable that before going hunting the hunters would draw or study pictures of animals and imagine a successful hunt.
To protect themselves, producers of consumer goods plaster their packages with warnings about every conceivable mishap.
The bar is stocked with every conceivable kind of premium liquor and has a cigar menu.
